methods.description: Analysis of structural features of flowers from various allergenic plant species to determine the number of stamens, average number of flowers per inflorescence, and per plant.

steps.description: Measurements were made of particular flower elements, the number of stamens in the androecium as well as the average number of flowers per inflorescence and per plant were determined in twenty herbaceous plants of each species.
steps.searchable_summary: measure flower elements and count stamens and flowers per inflorescence and plant
inputs: flower samples from 20 herbaceous plants of each species

steps.description: In the case of birch, the number of flowers per inflorescence was established based on the examination of 30 inflorescences sampled from different branches of three trees.
steps.searchable_summary: count flowers per inflorescence for birch from 30 samples
outputs: flower counts per inflorescence for birch

methods.description: Determination of pollen grain production and size for various plant species.

steps.description: The number of pollen grains produced per anther was determined and the obtained results were then recalculated per flower and per inflorescence, and in the case of the herbaceous plants, also per plant.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen grain count per anther and recalculate for flower and inflorescence
outputs: pollen grain counts per anther, flower, inflorescence, and plant

steps.description: Mature stamens were sampled for the investigation before anther dehiscence. Pollen grains were washed out of the thecae with 70% alcohol onto a microscope slide using a stereoscopic microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: sample mature stamens and wash pollen grains onto slide with alcohol

steps.description: The size of pollen grains of the studied plants was determined based on the dimensions of their polar (P) and equatorial (E) axes. 200 pollen grains were measured for each species.
steps.searchable_summary: measure polar and equatorial axes of 200 pollen grains per species

methods.description: Establishment of the period of occurrence of airborne pollen and pollen concentrations using a pollen trap.

steps.description: The period of occurrence of airborne pollen - the atmospheric pollen season - and pollen concentrations were established using a Lanzoni VPPS 2000 pollen trap.
steps.searchable_summary: use Lanzoni VPPS 2000 pollen trap to monitor pollen season and concentrations

steps.description: The study was conducted in Lublin in the years 2001-2007. The pollen trap was located on the roof of the building of the University of Life Sciences (51 0 14'37'' N and 22 0 32'25'' E) at a height of 18 m above ground.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct pollen monitoring in Lublin from 2001-2007 using rooftop pollen trap
